question: In the study by Mao et al., what type of performance metric was primarily emphasized when using LLMs to predict the progression from MCI to AD?

options.A: Precision, focusing on the proportion of true positive predictions over all positive predictions.
options.B: F1-Score, which balances precision and recall for a more comprehensive performance measure.
options.C: Recall, highlighting the model's capability to identify all actual cases of disease progression.
options.D: Accuracy, as a measure of the LLM's ability to correctly predict disease progression.

explanation: The knowledge graph path from Mao et al. to LLMs and then to Accuracy indicates that the primary performance metric used in the study was 'Accuracy'. This metric reflects the correctness of the LLM in predicting the progression from MCI to AD.
